payload.text: Es una app de finanzas personales con el método 50/30/20, en la intro  pide tu ingreso y puedes elegir entre mensual semanal o quincenal(solo la primera vez de uso), ahora al inicio normal que esté una racha de ahorro, un balance entre ingresos y gastos totales, poder anotar gastos, en otro apartado tenerlo dividido, gastos necesarios, gastos personales o deseos con el presupuesto dividido en cada uno, en otro apartado opciones para aprender a ahorrar y dar primeros pasos para invertir y tips y consejos

**Initiating Finance Planning**

I'm starting by establishing the initial income information - the amount and how often it comes in. This will be a one-time setup step, only occurring during onboarding.


**Defining Core Components**

I've been mapping out the core user interface components. I am planning the dashboard: the savings streak, and the balance visualization (income versus expenses). I'm also planning the budget breakdown with a 50/30/20 division, and a learning section with tips from the Gemini API. Plus, I need to add an expense entry section.



**Analyzing Data Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating the data, specifically how the income, expenses, and streak values will interact. I'm prioritizing the logic for fetching financial tips, and I'm designing the integration with Gemini, especially around prompt engineering for personalized advice. I've also added self-correction functionality using `localStorage` to persist the state.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Inter font.
React entry point using React 18 createRoot.
Global TypeScript types for the application.
Constants and shared configuration values.
Service for interacting with Gemini API to provide financial tips.
Initial setup screen for new users.
Main dashboard with balance and quick expense entry.
Detailed view of the 50/30/20 budget breakdown.
AI-driven financial tips and investment education center.
Main application component managing navigation and global state.

payload.text: En el componente BudgetDetails.tsx, añade un nuevo gráfico de barras que muestre el progreso de cada categoría (Necesidades, Deseos, Ahorro) contra su presupuesto. Usa los datos existentes de `splits` y `getSpent`. El gráfico debe ser interactivo y visualmente atractivo, utilizando los colores definidos en `CATEGORY_COLORS`.

**Constructing the Bar Chart**

I'm now integrating a bar chart into `BudgetDetails.tsx`. My focus is on visualizing budget progress by category (Needs, Wants, Savings). I'm leveraging the existing 'splits' for budget targets and 'getSpent' data for actual spending, which should make the implementation easier and the output more useful.


**Enhancing the Visualization**

I'm now focusing on making the bar chart interactive and visually appealing using `CATEGORY_COLORS` and the `recharts` library for consistency with the rest of the application. I'm importing necessary components for the chart, such as `BarChart`, `Bar`, `XAxis`, and so on. Currently, I'm refactoring the data structure to include `name`, `Presupuesto`, `Gastado`, and `color` for each category.

entries.description: Added a responsive Bar Chart to visualize budget vs. actual spending using Recharts, with custom styling and tooltips.

payload.text: En la sección 'Aprende y Crece', permite al usuario definir metas de ahorro personalizadas (ej. 'Comprar un coche', 'Vacaciones'). Muestra el progreso hacia estas metas y ofrece consejos de IA específicos para alcanzarlas.

**Developing Savings Goals Feature**

I'm currently focused on integrating a "Savings Goals" feature into `LearningCenter.tsx`. My aim is to let users define their personalized goals like "Buy a car" and visualize their progress. I also want to incorporate AI to provide smart recommendations.


**Refining Implementation Steps**

I've refined the implementation steps, starting with updating `types.ts` to include the `SavingsGoal` interface, which I've updated with an ID, name, target and current amounts. Next, I'm updating `App.tsx` to handle the goals state and persistence through localStorage. Then, I'm focusing on updating `LearningCenter.tsx` to display goals and pass them to the `geminiService.ts` to get personalized tips. Finally, I'm updating `geminiService.ts` to include goals information in prompts for tailored financial advice.

entries.description: Added SavingsGoal interface to the types definition.
Updated financial advice service to include personalized savings goals in the prompt for tailored tips.
Updated App component to manage savings goals state and persistence.
Implemented a new Savings Goals section with goal creation, progress tracking, and AI-powered tips integrated into the Learning Center.

payload.text: Que en el plan 50/30/20 se aplique mensual independientemente el tiempo que escojas al principio va a aparecer mensual y en chico el tiempo escogido y que al principio de todo en la intro cambia el título por ¡Bienvenido a zcorpion!

**Revising Onboarding Title**

I'm currently revising the onboarding title for the personal finance app. The focus is on a title change from "¡Bienvenido a Finanza503020!" to "¡Bienvenido a zcorpion!". This involves a simple text replacement, but it's a first step in a larger modification effort based on the 50/30/20 budget method.


**Refining Monthly Budget View**

I'm now focusing on how the 50/30/20 budget will be displayed and calculated, regardless of the user's initial income frequency. I'm prioritizing a monthly display in the "Plan" tab, with the original frequency subtly shown. I'm updating `App.tsx` to handle frequency conversions, and `BudgetDetails.tsx` to ensure monthly normalization. The monthly amount must be prominent in the UI, and I'm ensuring consistency in the dashboard view as well.


**Calculating Monthly Income**

I'm now refining the monthly income normalization within `BudgetDetails.tsx`. My focus is ensuring accurate calculations, regardless of the initial income frequency (weekly, biweekly, or monthly). I will use 4.33 for weekly and 2 for biweekly conversion. The primary display will be monthly, with the original frequency subtly noted. This means updating components to show the normalized monthly values. I'm focusing on ensuring data consistency between the plan view and the dashboard header.

entries.description: Changed the onboarding welcome message title to '¡Bienvenido a zcorpion!'.
Modified calculations to normalize income to monthly regardless of chosen frequency, and added UI display for original frequency.
Updated header summary text to show monthly calculation and original frequency context.

payload.text: Que el cálculo mensual se calcule la semana por 4 y la quincena por 2, y agrega un espacio de configuraciones donde puedas cerrar sesión y cambiar el color a modo oscuro, también un botón para quitar gastos y que en la racha para que pueda ser cumplida tienes que cumplir con tu meta de ahorro anotando la cantidad

**Refining Core Functionality**

I'm currently focused on refining the monthly calculation logic for expenses; I want to ensure it's as accurate as possible. I've also started working on the settings section, planning to include logout and dark mode toggle functionality. Next up: deleting expenses. Finally, I'll be working on linking the "savings streak" to a specific savings goal.


**Implementing Key Adjustments**

I've just finished revising the income calculation for weekly and bi-weekly entries; I've streamlined the logic to be a multiple of the income instead of a less-intuitive fraction. I'm also preparing the settings space. Implementing the delete button within the transaction list is next. Then, I'll update the streak logic to require users to meet their savings targets. Finally, I'll add the Dark Mode toggle and Logout functionality.

entries.description: Implemented dark mode, logout functionality, modified monthly calculation logic, added expense deletion, and updated streak logic based on savings activity.
New component for application settings, dark mode toggle, and logout.
Updated Dashboard to support dark mode and display updated streak logic visuals.
Modified monthly calculation logic (Weekly x4, Biweekly x2), implemented expense deletion, and added dark mode support.
Updated LearningCenter to support dark mode visuals.

payload.text: En la intro agrega una opción de la divisa, de pesos mexicanos, euro y dólar. También que después puedas cambiar el tiempo que escogiste al principio en configuraciones. Que la racha de ahorro sea un botón que te mande para anotar y cumplir la racha

**Expanding Currency Options**

I've been focusing on expanding the currency options for "zcorpion". I'm adding MXN, EUR, and USD support. Furthermore, I've been working on a settings option for users to adjust their income frequency (weekly, biweekly, monthly) in the Settings component.


**Implementing Streak Integration**

I'm now prioritizing the "🔥" streak card implementation. This includes making it clickable to navigate to the expense form and auto-focusing on the "Savings" category. I'm updating `Dashboard.tsx` to handle this. I am also working on `App.tsx` to handle frequency and currency changes from settings. Calculation logic has been updated to reflect the new frequency settings.


**Refining Streak Button**

I'm now refining the streak button's behavior. I'll make the streak card a button within `Dashboard.tsx`, setting the expense form category to "SAVINGS" when clicked. My plan is to update `types.ts` for currency, add currency selection to `Onboarding.tsx`, and frequency/currency updates to `Settings.tsx`. `App.tsx` will manage profile updates, and `Dashboard.tsx` now uses the clickable streak card that pre-selects SAVINGS. I'll also add labels/symbols to currency options.

entries.description: Ensure Currency is part of UserProfile (already exists, but making sure it's used correctly).
Added currency labels and symbols.
Added currency selection to the onboarding process.
Streak card is now a button that pre-selects 'Savings' in the quick entry form. Added currency symbol support.
Added options to change income frequency, amount, and currency in the Settings component.
Updated onboarding logic to include currency. Added profile update handler for settings.

payload.text: En el componente Dashboard.tsx, modifica la lógica de la racha de ahorro. Asegúrate de que la racha solo se incremente si el usuario registra un gasto en la categoría 'Ahorro' dependiendo de el tiempo elegido al principio y si excede de el tiempo la racha vuelve a cero, también desde que inicia la racha que se vea el tiempo que queda para no perderla

**Refining Streak Criteria**

I'm currently focused on refining the streak logic within `Dashboard.tsx`, and I anticipate needing to adjust `App.tsx` as well. The key requirement is that a streak increment only occurs with a "Savings" (Ahorro) category entry. It's becoming clearer that the streak's state hinges on the active user.


**Revising Streak Definitions**

I'm now clarifying the definitions of the streaks, driven by the user's selected frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly). My understanding is that the streak represents consecutive *periods* of saving, not daily savings. If weekly, it's weeks in a row saved. If monthly, it's months in a row saved. I'm moving toward applying this new understanding to the logic in `App.tsx`. I will also show a countdown to not lose the streak in the UI.


**Implementing Streak Expiration**

I'm now implementing the streak expiration logic, driven by the frequency setting (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly). My update is I've decided to explicitly track the `lastSavingsDate`. The `App.tsx` useEffect hook will handle the expiration checks and I will display a countdown in `Dashboard.tsx`. I will also check for diffDays to reset the streak.


**Clarifying Streak Increment Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the streak increment rules. Considering the user's specific feedback, the streak should advance only upon a "Savings" (Ahorro) category entry *within* a defined period (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly). Multiple savings entries within a period don't increase the streak; it's about consecutive periods. The "Racha" (Streak) is based on consistency, not daily activity, as the user says. I will adapt the `App.tsx` logic to reflect this. I will also make an expiration date and countdown.


**Updating Streak Mechanics**

I'm now focusing on a simplified streak increment rule: If a user saves, and it's been more than a day since their last save, the streak increments. If it's been more than the period duration (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly) since the last save, the streak resets to zero. I'll use `lastLoginDate` as the last successful saving registration date. I'm updating `types.ts` to add `lastSavingDate` to `UserProfile`, revising `App.tsx` for streak reset on load and increment on save, and refining `Dashboard.tsx` to display streak information and time remaining.


**Calculating Expiration Logic**

My current focus is on calculating the expiration logic, considering the selected frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly). I'm planning to update `App.tsx` and `Dashboard.tsx` to handle the streak increment and expiration, as well as ensure the countdown display is accurate. I'm building out the new code as we speak, and I'll keep the logic in `Settings.tsx` consistent, so that if the user changes the frequency, it does not negatively impact the streak. The user wants the streak to take them to the "anotar" area, so I'll polish that too. I am writing code now.

entries.description: Updated streak logic to expire based on frequency (7 days for weekly, 14 for biweekly, 30 for monthly) and reset on load if expired.
Modified streak card to show time remaining before expiration based on frequency. Integrated visual countdown indicator.
